title: lithocarpus craibianus
intro: Lithocarpus craibianus, also known as the Craib's tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that can help to alleviate various health issues. The health benefits of Lithocarpus craibianus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. In traditional herbal medicine, the bark of the tree is typically used to make tea or tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of the plant's medicinal properties.
